title: Kottekkad Temple
tagline: Kottekkad Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kottekkad Temple is a Hindu temple situated about 8 km from Thrissur, Kerala, India.
overview.description: Kottekkad Temple is a Hindu temple situated about 8 km from Thrissur, Kerala, India. It has "pratishtas" of Bhagavathi, Shiva, Aanganear (Hanuman), Ayyappan, Nagathanmar, Subhramaniyan (Murugan), and Bhrahmarakshassu.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Marutharode
topPlaces.description: Marutharode (also spelled Marutharoad) is a census town, gram panchayat and suburb of Palakkad city in the Palakkad district, state of Kerala, India. It is one of Panchayat which is supposed to be part of proposed Palakkad Municipal Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Chandranagar, Palakkad
topPlaces.description: Chandranagar is a commercial and residential area in Palakkad city, Kerala, India. Chandranagar housing Society, founded in 1956, is the first housing colony in the state and one of the largest.

topPlaces.name: Puthur (Palakkad)
topPlaces.description: Puthur is a residential area in the Palakkad district, in the state of Kerala, India. Puthur comprises the wards 12 and 13 of Palakkad Municipality.

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya, Kanjikode
topPlaces.description: Kendriya Vidyalaya, Kanjikode, officially Kendriya Vidyalaya No.2 Palakkad, is a Senior Secondary School (Std 1-12) affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE), New Delhi and functions under the purview of Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan (KVS), an autonomous government body. In 2023, the Vidyalaya was selected for the Pradhan Mantri Schools for Rising India (PM SHRI) scheme launched by the Ministry of Education.

topPlaces.name: Stadium Bus Stand Palakkad
topPlaces.description: Stadium Bus Stand is located on the heart of the Palakkad City, near the Indira Gandhi Municipal Stadium in Kerala, India. Stadium Bus Stand is the largest Private Bus Stand in the city of Palakkad.

topPlaces.name: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Palakkad
topPlaces.description: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Palakkad, locally known as JNV Malampuzha, is a boarding, co-educational school in Palakkad district of Kerala state in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti, an autonomous body under the ministry.

topPlaces.name: Walayar River
topPlaces.description: Walayar River (also called Velliyar River or Valliyar River) is one of the tributaries of the river Kalpathipuzha.It originates in the Sirumalai hills. The Kalpathipuzha is one of the main tributaries of the Bharathapuzha River, the second-longest river in Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Sultanpet
topPlaces.description: Roman Catholic Diocese of Sultanpet (Latin: Dioecesis Sultanpetensis) is a Roman Catholic diocese consisting of Palakkad district, Kerala, India erected from the division of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Coimbatore and Roman Catholic Diocese of Calicut, and a suffragan of the Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Verapoly. Rev.

Puthur is a residential area in the Palakkad district, in the state of Kerala, India. Puthur comprises the wards 12 and 13 of Palakkad Municipality.

Kendriya Vidyalaya, Kanjikode, officially Kendriya Vidyalaya No.2 Palakkad, is a Senior Secondary School (Std 1-12) affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE), New Delhi and functions under the purview of Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan (KVS), an autonomous government body. In 2023, the Vidyalaya was selected for the Pradhan Mantri Schools for Rising India (PM SHRI) scheme launched by the Ministry of Education.
Stadium Bus Stand is located on the heart of the Palakkad City, near the Indira Gandhi Municipal Stadium in Kerala, India. Stadium Bus Stand is the largest Private Bus Stand in the city of Palakkad.

Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Palakkad, locally known as JNV Malampuzha, is a boarding, co-educational school in Palakkad district of Kerala state in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti, an autonomous body under the ministry.

Walayar River (also called Velliyar River or Valliyar River) is one of the tributaries of the river Kalpathipuzha.It originates in the Sirumalai hills. The Kalpathipuzha is one of the main tributaries of the Bharathapuzha River, the second-longest river in Kerala, India.

Roman Catholic Diocese of Sultanpet (Latin: Dioecesis Sultanpetensis) is a Roman Catholic diocese consisting of Palakkad district, Kerala, India erected from the division of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Coimbatore and Roman Catholic Diocese of Calicut, and a suffragan of the Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Verapoly. Rev.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kottekkad Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kottekkad Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kottekkad Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Coimbatore International Airport (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (38 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kottekkad Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kottekkad Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kottekkad Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kottekkad Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kottekkad Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Marutharode, Chandranagar, Palakkad, Puthur (Palakkad), Kendriya Vidyalaya, Kanjikode, Stadium Bus Stand Palakkad.
